name: Daniel to Revelation
summary: The sacred scriptures and writings that form the foundation of Christian faith and theology.
structure: Judgment Typology - From historical furnace to eschatological judgment
influence: Shaped Christian understanding of trial, persecution, and divine refining
icon:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 64 64">
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="28.8" fill="#9370DB" opacity="0.2"/>
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="19.2" fill="none" stroke="#9370DB" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,5"/>
  <text x="32" y="44.8" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22.4" fill="#9370DB">✨</text>
</svg>
description: Daniel to Revelation is an important sacred text in christian tradition.
author: Daniel (Prophet), John (Apostle)

keyMyths.title: Daniel's Visions
keyMyths.description: Daniel receives a series of prophetic visions, including those of four beasts representing successive kingdoms and the coming of a divine kingdom that will endure forever. These visions are interpreted by angels and provide a framework for understanding the course of history and the ultimate triumph of God.

keyMyths.title: The Fiery Furnace
keyMyths.description: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego are thrown into a fiery furnace for refusing to worship a golden statue erected by King Nebuchadnezzar. They are miraculously saved by God, who sends an angel to protect them from the flames, demonstrating the power of faith and divine intervention.

keyMyths.title: The Handwriting on the Wall
keyMyths.description: During a feast thrown by King Belshazzar, a mysterious hand appears and writes on the wall, prophesying the downfall of the Babylonian kingdom. Daniel interprets the writing, revealing God's judgment against Belshazzar for his impiety and predicting the imminent conquest of Babylon.

keyMyths.title: The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse
keyMyths.description: John witnesses a scroll with seven seals, and as each seal is broken, a horseman appears, representing conquest, war, famine, and death. These horsemen are harbingers of the end times, signaling a period of tribulation and divine judgment upon the earth.

keyMyths.title: The New Jerusalem
keyMyths.description: John envisions a new heaven and a new earth, with the New Jerusalem descending from heaven, a city of pure gold and precious stones. This represents the ultimate fulfillment of God's promises, where believers will dwell in eternal peace and communion with God, free from suffering and death.

sources.name: The Book of Daniel
sources.description: A prophetic book in the Hebrew Bible (Tanakh) and the Christian Old Testament, attributed to the prophet Daniel, containing narratives and visions.
sources.title: The Book of Daniel

sources.name: The Book of Revelation
sources.description: The final book of the New Testament, also known as the Apocalypse, attributed to John of Patmos, containing symbolic visions of the end times.
sources.title: The Book of Revelation

sources.name: Collins, John J. *Daniel: A Commentary on the Book of Daniel*.
sources.description: A comprehensive academic commentary on the Book of Daniel, exploring its historical context, literary structure, and theological themes.
sources.title: Collins, John J. *Daniel: A Commentary on the Book of Daniel*.

sources.name: Bauckham, Richard. *The Theology of the Book of Revelation*.
sources.description: A scholarly analysis of the theological message and significance of the Book of Revelation.
sources.title: Bauckham, Richard. *The Theology of the Book of Revelation*.

sources.name: Metzger, Bruce M. *Breaking the Code: Understanding the Book of Revelation*.
sources.description: An accessible introduction to the Book of Revelation, explaining its symbols and themes in a clear and concise manner.
sources.title: Metzger, Bruce M. *Breaking the Code: Understanding the Book of Revelation*.
